_doc: Operator-maintained inventory of GitHub Personal Access Tokens used
with this repository. Read by scripts/check_security_posture.py on a
monthly cron to flag stale or unbounded PATs (#227, 2026-05-26).
Why this file exists: GitHub does NOT expose a list-my-PATs API. To
audit token freshness mechanically, we maintain this file as source
of truth and update it on every PAT rotation/creation.
Schema per entry:
name              short label (visible in the PAT's GitHub UI)
created_at        ISO YYYY-MM-DD
expires_at        ISO YYYY-MM-DD, or null (forever — flagged)
last_used_at      ISO YYYY-MM-DD, the operator's last touch date.
Update when you next use the token; the cron
flags entries where this is > 60 days ago.
scopes            list of strings (the GitHub PAT scopes).
where_used        free-text: which workflow/script consumes it.
Audit thresholds (in scripts/check_security_posture.py):
- expires_at == null               → flagged (rotate to a finite
expiry)
- expires_at > 90 days from today  → flagged (cap at 90)
- last_used_at > 60 days ago       → flagged (consider revoking)

tokens.name: (placeholder) — populate when you next rotate a PAT
tokens.where_used: Placeholder so the cron has a non-empty list to validate against. Replace with the real PAT(s) when you next rotate. If you have zero PATs in use, delete this entry and the audit will report 0 tokens.
